messages.content: I pulled the docs on our vector-database options (a few twice by accident). Which one should we pick for a Postgres-heavy stack, and why?

messages.name: fetch_doc
messages.content:
# Pinecone

Managed vector database, serverless and pod-based tiers. Metadata filtering, hybrid search, namespaces for multi-tenancy.

Pricing scales with stored vectors and queries per second; the managed tier bundles backups, replication, and a dashboard. Latency at p95 stays under 50ms for the recommended index settings. SDKs are available for Python and TypeScript. Pricing scales with stored vectors and queries per second; the managed tier bundles backups, replication, and a dashboard. messages.content:
# Weaviate

Open-source vector DB with a GraphQL API, built-in modules for embeddings, and a managed cloud option.

Pricing scales with stored vectors and queries per second; the managed tier bundles backups, replication, and a dashboard. Latency at p95 stays under 50ms for the recommended index settings. messages.name: fetch_doc
messages.content:
# Qdrant

Open-source vector search engine in Rust, payload filtering, on-disk storage, and a managed cloud.

Pricing scales with stored vectors and queries per second; the managed tier bundles backups, replication, and a dashboard. Latency at p95 stays under 50ms for the recommended index settings. # pgvector

A Postgres extension adding a vector type and ANN indexes; reuses the database you already run.

Pricing scales with stored vectors and queries per second; the managed tier bundles backups, replication, and a dashboard. Latency at p95 stays under 50ms for the recommended index settings.
